title: Fix role registration failure across admin/driver/traveller

requirements.text: Diagnose and fix the end-to-end registration flow so that a signed-in user can successfully register as Traveller or Driver from the Register page without the backend trapping, and the UI transitions to the correct dashboard after a successful registration.

quotes: I can't able to register as any role

acceptanceCriteria: When signed in with Internet Identity, clicking “Register as Traveller” results in a backend RegistrationStatus success and the app navigates to the Traveller dashboard.
When signed in with Internet Identity, registering as Driver with a valid bus number results in RegistrationStatus success and the app navigates to the Driver dashboard.
If registration fails, the UI shows the exact failure message returned by the backend (RegistrationStatus.failure) and does not navigate away.
No registration attempt results in an unhandled exception or blank screen in the frontend.

requirements.text: Update backend registration methods to avoid using Runtime.trap for expected registration errors; return RegistrationStatus.failure with an English message instead, so the frontend can display actionable errors (including for admin registration).

quotes: I can't able to register as any role

acceptanceCriteria: Calling adminRegister/driverRegister/travellerRegister with invalid inputs or insufficient permissions returns #failure("...") and does not trap.
Frontend receives a structured RegistrationStatus in all expected failure cases and can show toast errors without relying on thrown exceptions.

requirements.text: Fix backend role/profile values so the frontend can reliably determine whether the registered user is an admin, driver, or traveller based on getCallerUserProfile output (including aligning the backend’s UserProfile.role encoding with what the generated frontend types represent).

quotes: I can't able to register as any role

acceptanceCriteria: After successful Traveller registration, getCallerUserProfile returns a non-null profile that the frontend interprets as a traveller and routes to the Traveller dashboard.
After successful Driver registration, getCallerUserProfile returns a non-null profile with a non-null busNumber and the frontend routes to the Driver dashboard.
After successful Admin registration (when permitted), getCallerUserProfile indicates an admin role and the frontend routes to the Admin dashboard.
App.tsx does not compare role values in a way that can never be true for the actual candid-generated role type.

requirements.text: Enable a workable admin registration experience: if the system has no admin configured yet, allow the first admin to be created; otherwise, restrict admin creation per existing authorization rules and return a friendly failure message when the caller is not allowed.

quotes: I can't able to register as any role

acceptanceCriteria: If there is no admin configured in access control state, a signed-in user can register as admin successfully using the existing Register page admin tab.
If an admin already exists, non-admin users attempting admin registration receive a non-trapping RegistrationStatus.failure explaining they are not authorized.
If an admin already exists, an admin user can register another admin (or self-register as admin if that is the intended behavior) per the updated rule set, without traps.

constraints: Respect the single-actor backend architecture: all Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo.
Do not edit any frontend files under frontend/src/components/ui or any paths listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Use English for all user-facing messages (toasts, alerts, backend failure strings).
nonGoals: Adding third-party authentication providers (only Internet Identity is supported).
Adding external databases or off-chain storage.
